title: Photos: ‘No Kings’ sparks protests in Europe
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Thousands protest in solidarity with the 'No Kings' movement across Europe, including in Madrid, Paris, Rome, and Amsterdam.
'No Kings' protests originated in the U.S., opposing Donald Trump since his second term began in January 2025.
Over 3,000 protests are planned in the U.S., with Massachusetts hosting more than 160 events, second only to California.

title: Bravo Just Dropped the first full “Real Housewives of Rhode Island” Trailer. Here’s everything we learned.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: 'The Real Housewives of Rhode Island' premieres April 2 at 9 p.m., marking the first Housewives series in New England.
The trailer features Alicia Carmody, a Providence resident, and Liz McGraw, the self-proclaimed 'Cannabis Queen' of Rhode Island.
The show explores Rhode Islanders' family ties, community roots, and unique local social dynamics as depicted in the trailer.

title: Hampden County Sheriff plans dozens of job cuts
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Hampden County Sheriff's Department to cut 50 jobs, saving up to $4 million after $26.5 million budget reduction.
Patrols in Springfield, Holyoke, and other towns will be reduced; specialized units like dive and drone teams scaled back.
Cuts stem from statewide funding issues, with some changes effective immediately and others phased over months.

title: Quincy approves money for new firefighting gear after PFAS found in old gear
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Quincy city council approved $2.65 million to replace firefighters' gear containing PFAS with safer alternatives.
The current gear was believed PFAS-free until testing revealed contamination; Quincy plans to buy two new PFAS-free sets per firefighter.
Massachusetts law will ban knowingly selling firefighting gear with PFAS starting January 2027.

title: With his new doc  ‘Henry David Thoreau,’ Don Henley returns to Walden
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Don Henley, executive producer of 'Henry David Thoreau', discusses the new documentary at Concord Carlisle Regional High School.
The Walden Woods Project, founded by Henley, aims to preserve Thoreau's legacy and promote environmental stewardship.
The documentary, airing on PBS, features narrations by George Clooney and performances by Meryl Streep, Jeff Goldblum, and others.

title: World Cup friendly match at Gillette was an ‘amazing’ off-day field trip for the Celtics
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Boston Celtics attended a friendly soccer match between France and Brazil at Gillette Stadium on Thursday.
French forward Kylian Mbappe scored to help France win 2-1, swapping jerseys with Celtics rookie Hugo Gonzalez.
Coach Joe Mazzulla highlighted similarities between soccer and basketball during the outing focused on team bonding.

title: Payton Pritchard’s huge night leads short-handed Celtics over streaking Hawks: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Payton Pritchard scored 36 points leading the Celtics to a 109-102 win over the Atlanta Hawks.
Jayson Tatum played 36 minutes, contributing 25 points despite shooting 8-for-24 from the field.
Jordan Walsh returned to the lineup, adding 5 points and 3 blocks in the Celtics' defensive effort.

title: Why did Jayson Tatum come back this season? The Celtics’ success had ‘a lot’ to do with it.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics this season after recovering from a ruptured Achilles' tendon sustained ten months ago.
Tatum's decision to return was influenced by the Celtics' strong performance and championship contention during his absence.
Despite ongoing recovery, Tatum's defensive rebounding and versatility remain strong, averaging 9.2 rebounds per game.

title: Police: Man killed in Lowell apartment complex shooting
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A man was fatally shot during an altercation at a Lowell apartment on Pawtucket Boulevard.
The shooting involved two armed men who knew each other from the building, according to investigators.
The case is under investigation by the Middlesex DA’s Office, State Police, and Lowell Police.

title: Report: Connecticut Sun to relocate to Houston with purchase by Rockets owners
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Connecticut Sun will relocate to Houston for the 2027 WNBA season under new ownership by the Fertitta family.
The team will be renamed the Houston Comets, reviving a former WNBA franchise disbanded in 2008.
The sale of the Sun to the Rockets' owners reportedly sets a WNBA record at $300 million.
